title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe: Make Authentic Bo Lo Bao at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get that signature crispy topping and soft bread!
longTailKeywords: how to make Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Topping
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Chinese Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden crispy topping.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, fluffy Pineapple Buns with their iconic sweet, crumbly crust – perfect for breakfast or a snack!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved classic from Hong Kong bakeries, famous for their soft, sweet bread crowned with a crunchy, sugary crust that resembles a pineapple's skin. Despite the name, there's no actual pineapple in these delightful treats, just pure comfort in every bite. They are perfect enjoyed warm with a cup of milk tea or coffee.

ingredients.notes: optional, for richer flavor, for the topping

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine bread flour, 30g sugar, salt, and instant dry yeast. Gradually add warm milk and the beaten egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter and continue kneading for another 5 minutes until the dough is very smooth and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This is passive time.

instructions.title: Prepare the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough proofs, cream 60g softened butter and 60g sugar in a medium bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk and milk powder (if using) until well combined. Gradually add the all-purpose flour and mix until a soft, crumbly dough forms.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions (about 60g each). Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the shaped dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Divide the topping into 8 equal portions and roll each into a ball. Flatten each ball into a disc about 3-4 inches in diameter between two sheets of parchment paper.

instructions.title: Apply Topping & Second Proofing
instructions.text: Carefully peel one flattened topping disc from the parchment paper and gently place it over a dough ball, pressing lightly to adhere. Repeat for all buns. Brush the visible dough parts (not the topping) with a light egg wash. Score a crosshatch pattern on the topping with a knife. Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. This is passive time.

instructions.title: Bake the Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Bake the pineapple buns for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the bread is cooked through. If the topping browns too quickly, you can loosely tent with foil.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Pineapple buns are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven, perhaps with a slice of butter inserted into the middle for an extra treat.

tips: For an extra fluffy dough, ensure your milk is warm (not hot!) to activate the yeast properly, around 40-45°C (105-115°F).
If you dont have milk powder for the topping, you can omit it. The topping will still be delicious, just slightly less rich in flavor.
Pineapple buns are always best eaten fresh and warm. If not serving immediately, store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat briefly in the oven for best texture.
article:
Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved staple of Cantonese bakeries worldwide. With their signature golden, crumbly, and sweet topping resembling the skin of a pineapple, these buns offer a delightful contrast of textures that has captivated palates for generations. Despite their name, there's no actual pineapple in them – just pure, comforting deliciousness. Imagine biting into a fluffy, soft bread roll topped with a slightly crisp, sugary crust that shatters gently with each bite. It's an experience that transports you straight to the bustling streets of Hong Kong. If you've ever dreamt of recreating this magic at home, our comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe is here to guide you through every step, transforming your kitchen into a professional-grade Chinese bakery.

## What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?

At its heart, a Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) is a sweet bread roll characterized by its iconic crunchy, sugary, and often slightly crumbly topping. The name "pineapple" comes purely from the appearance of this topping, which, when properly scored and baked, develops a distinctive crackled pattern akin to a pineapple's rough exterior. Originating from Hong Kong, these buns are a quintessential item in dim sum restaurants and Chinese bakeries across the globe. The magic lies in the exquisite textural contrast: a rich, tender, and airy yeast-leavened bread base perfectly complemented by the crisp, cookie-like crust. This crust is typically made from a mixture of flour, sugar, butter, and egg, creating a delicate shell that encases the soft interior. While traditionally enjoyed plain, perhaps with a thick slice of cold butter tucked inside (known as "bolo yau"), modern variations often include sweet or savory fillings, making them incredibly versatile.

## Ingredients for Homemade Pineapple Buns

Gathering the right ingredients is the first step in mastering this delightful Pineapple Bun Recipe. Quality ingredients make a noticeable difference in the final product.

For the **Dough**:
*   **All-Purpose or Bread Flour:** 300g (about 2 ½ cups). Bread flour provides a chewier texture due to higher protein, but all-purpose works well too.
*   **Granulated Sugar:** 50g (about ¼ cup). For sweetness and to feed the yeast.
*   **Salt:** ½ teaspoon. Balances flavors.
*   **Instant Dry Yeast:** 5g (about 1 ½ teaspoons). For leavening.
*   **Warm Milk:** 120ml (about ½ cup). Around 100-110°F (38-43°C) to activate the yeast.
*   **Large Egg:** 1, at room temperature. For richness and structure.
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 40g (about 3 tablespoons), softened. Adds tenderness and flavor.

For the **Pineapple Topping**:
*   **All-Purpose Flour:** 100g (about ¾ cup).
*   **Granulated Sugar:** 60g (about ¼ cup + 2 tablespoons). For sweetness and crispness.
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 50g (about 3 ½ tablespoons), cold and cubed. Crucial for the crumbly texture.
*   **Large Egg Yolk:** 1. Adds richness and helps bind.
*   **Milk Powder (optional):** 1 tablespoon. Enhances flavor and color.
*   **Baking Powder (optional):** ¼ teaspoon. For a slightly lighter, crisper topping.
*   **Water or Milk:** 1-2 teaspoons, if needed, to bring the dough together.

For the **Egg Wash**:
*   **Large Egg:** 1, whisked with 1 tablespoon water or milk. For a golden sheen.

## Step-by-Step Pineapple Bun Instructions

Now, let's dive into the detailed steps of our Pineapple Bun Recipe, transforming simple ingredients into bakery-worthy treats.

1.  **Prepare the Dough:**
    *   In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, salt, and yeast.
    *   In a separate bowl, whisk the warm milk and egg.
    *   Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
    *   Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth.
    *   Incorporate the softened butter and continue kneading for another 5-10 minutes until the dough is elastic and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through without tearing).
    *   Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

2.  **Prepare the Topping:**
    *   In a medium bowl, cream together the cold cubed butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
    *   Beat in the egg yolk.
    *   Add the flour, milk powder (if using), and baking powder (if using). Mix until just combined and a crumbly dough forms. If it's too dry, add a teaspoon of water or milk.
    *   Gather the topping dough, flatten it slightly, wrap in plastic, and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

3.  **Shape the Buns:**
    *   Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air.
    *   Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions (about 60-70g each). Roll each portion into a smooth ball.
    *   Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.
    *   Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof for a second time in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

4.  **Apply the Topping:**
    *   Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
    *   Remove the chilled topping dough from the fridge. Divide it into the same number of portions as your buns.
    *   Working with one piece at a time, roll it out thinly between two pieces of parchment paper into a circle large enough to cover the top of a bun (about 3-4 inches in diameter).
    *   Carefully peel off one piece of parchment and drape the topping circle over a proofed bun. Gently press the edges down to secure it to the bun.
    *   Using a sharp knife, score a cross-hatch or grid pattern onto the topping, being careful not to cut too deep into the bread dough. This creates the "pineapple" effect.
    *   Repeat for all buns.

5.  **Bake the Buns:**
    *   Brush the tops of the buns (specifically the topping) with the egg wash.
    *   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through.
    *   Transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly.

## Tips for the Perfect Crispy Topping

Achieving that signature crispy, crumbly topping is key to an authentic Pineapple Bun Recipe. Here are a few tips to ensure your topping is perfect:

*   **Cold Butter is Your Friend:** For the topping, always use cold, cubed butter. This helps create a crumbly, shortbread-like texture. If the butter is too soft, the topping will be greasy and won't hold its shape as well.
*   **Don't Overmix the Topping:** Mix the topping ingredients just until they come together. Overmixing develops gluten, which can make the topping tough rather than crumbly.
*   **Chill the Topping Dough:** Chilling the topping dough makes it much easier to handle and prevents it from melting too quickly when rolled out. It also helps the topping maintain its structure and crispness during baking.
*   **Roll Thinly and Evenly:** Aim for a consistent thickness when rolling out the topping. Too thick, and it might be dense; too thin, and it might tear. Using parchment paper makes rolling and transferring much easier.
*   **Score with Confidence:** The scoring isn't just for aesthetics; it helps the topping expand and crackle in the oven, enhancing the "pineapple" look and crispiness. Use a sharp knife and make confident, even cuts.
*   **Egg Wash for Shine and Color:** A good egg wash not only gives your buns a beautiful golden sheen but also contributes to the topping's appealing color and slight crispness.

## Serving & Storage Suggestions

The best way to enjoy your homemade Pineapple Bun Recipe is undoubtedly fresh from the oven, still warm. The aroma alone is intoxicating, and the topping is at its crispiest, while the bread inside is at its softest.

*   **Classic Serving:** The most iconic way to serve a Pineapple Bun is to slice it horizontally and tuck a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter inside. The warmth of the bun melts the butter, creating a heavenly sweet and savory, rich experience. This is known as "Bo Lo Yau" (Pineapple Bun with Butter).
*   **Other Pairings:** They are excellent with a cup of hot Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or simply on their own as a delightful snack or breakfast item.
*   **Storage:** Store leftover pineapple buns in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. The topping will soften over time, but the bun will remain tender.
*   **Reheating:** To revive the crispiness of the topping and softness of the bun, reheat them in a toaster oven or conventional oven at 300°F (150°C) for 5-10 minutes, or until warmed through. Microwaving will make the bun soft but won't restore the topping's crispness.
*   **Freezing:** You can freeze baked and cooled pineapple buns for up to 1-2 months. Wrap them individually in plastic wrap, then place them in a freezer-safe bag. Thaw at room temperature and reheat as above. You can also freeze unbaked, shaped buns after their second proofing; simply transfer them directly from the freezer to a preheated oven, adding a few extra minutes to the baking time.

## Variations: Sweet & Savory Pineapple Buns

While the classic is irresistible, this versatile Pineapple Bun Recipe can be adapted with various fillings to create new and exciting flavor profiles. The beauty of the pineapple topping is that it pairs wonderfully with both sweet and savory surprises hidden within the fluffy bread.

**Sweet Variations:**

*   **Custard Cream:** A rich, silky smooth custard cream filling is a popular choice, adding an extra layer of sweetness and moistness.
*   **Red Bean Paste:** A traditional Chinese favorite, sweet red bean paste offers an earthy, subtly sweet complement to the bun.
*   **Coconut Filling:** Shredded coconut mixed with sugar and butter creates a fragrant and slightly chewy filling that's incredibly satisfying.
*   **Taro Paste:** A delicate, slightly sweet purple taro paste provides a unique flavor and beautiful color.

**Savory Variations:**

*   **BBQ Pork (Char Siu Bao):** This is perhaps the most famous savory variation. Diced char siu (Chinese BBQ pork) in a sweet and savory sauce is a sublime pairing with the pineapple bun's crust.
*   **Ham and Cheese:** A simple yet delicious combination, diced ham and shredded cheese (like cheddar or mozzarella) can be baked into the center for a comforting, hearty bun.
*   **Sausage:** Sliced Chinese sausage or even a hot dog can be nestled into the dough before baking for a quick and savory snack.

To incorporate a filling, simply flatten each dough ball after the first proofing, place about 1-2 tablespoons of your chosen filling in the center, and carefully pinch the edges together to seal the filling inside before reshaping it into a ball. Then proceed with the second proofing and topping application as per the original Pineapple Bun Recipe.

Embark on this culinary adventure with our detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe and bring a piece of Hong Kong's bakery tradition into your kitchen. The effort is well worth it for the incredible aroma and taste of freshly baked Bo Lo Bao!
